blocks.text: "vậy hả" means "really? / is that so?" — used to express surprise, mild disbelief, or to acknowledge new information casually. It is placed at the end of a sentence, often as a short reaction on its own.
You can add the second person's pronoun after it to soften the reaction and keep it polite / respectful toward the listener.

blocks.vi: Anh là người Mỹ. – Vậy hả anh?
Hôm nay cô ấy không đi làm. – Vậy hả chị?
blocks.en: She's not going to work today. – Really?
